name: Gundersen
description_short: Member-owned credit union in La Crosse, WI offering checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, and digital banking services to local members.
description_long:
Gundersen Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union based in La Crosse, Wisconsin. The institution is federally insured by the NCUA and operates as a traditional credit union serving its membership community. As of April 1st, 2026, Gundersen Credit Union merged with Dairyland Power Credit Union, expanding its member base and service capabilities. The credit union has been operating long enough to celebrate its 55th Annual Meeting, indicating decades of community presence and stability.

Gundersen Credit Union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, mortgage services, and home loans. The institution provides competitive lending rates, advertising vehicle loan rates as low as 4.64% APR. Members have access to deposit products like share accounts and share certificates (traditional credit union terminology), online banking, mobile app services, eStatements, and courtesy pay features. The credit union also offers shared branching access, expanding member convenience beyond their physical locations.

Gundersen's recent digital banking infrastructure upgrade distinguishes them from legacy competitors. They have invested in modernizing their online banking platform and launched a new mobile app designed for contemporary user experience. The credit union actively communicates with members through multiple channels and maintains multilingual support (English and Spanish). Their recent merger with Dairyland Power Credit Union suggests growth and organizational confidence.

As a credit union, Gundersen operates under different regulatory frameworks than banks and typically returns profits to members rather than shareholders. However, membership eligibility may be restricted to specific communities or employer groups, which is typical for credit unions but limits accessibility. The website content provided focuses on product announcements and basic service descriptions rather than detailed rate comparisons or comprehensive product specifications, which may require direct contact for complete information.
pros: Competitive auto loan rates as low as 4.64% APR advertised on website
Recently upgraded digital banking platform and new mobile app for modern user experience
Federally insured NCUA member providing consumer protection and stability
Shared branching access expands physical location availability beyond main office
Member-owned not-for-profit structure returns profits to members rather than external shareholders
Multilingual support available in English and Spanish
Recent merger with Dairyland Power Credit Union suggests organizational growth and strength
cons: Membership eligibility may be restricted based on employer affiliation or geographic location, limiting accessibility
Website provides limited rate comparison details and product specifications; requires member contact for comprehensive information
Limited physical branch presence (primary location appears to be La Crosse, WI area) unlike larger national banks
Routing number visible on homepage (291881177) but no clear explanation of membership requirements or eligibility criteria
No mention of specific promotional rates or incentives beyond vehicle loans; other product rates not disclosed on website
best_for: La Crosse, Wisconsin residents and eligible community members seeking local credit union relationship
Consumers prioritizing digital banking experience with modern mobile app and online platforms
Borrowers shopping for competitive auto loan rates from member-owned institution
Members valuing NCUA insurance protection and non-profit credit union structure
diagnosis: Gundersen Credit Union is best for La Crosse area residents and eligible community members seeking traditional credit union services with recently modernized digital banking capabilities. Primary caveat: membership eligibility appears restricted (employer or community-based), and detailed rate information requires direct contact rather than being transparently posted online.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
